rulings.Case Title: GARY HUBBELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Albay Construction Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, August 20, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court¿s own motion to August 31 to give defendant an opportunity to file reply papers if its wishes to do so. Any reply papers must be filed by August 25. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY HUBBELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Todd Shipyard Corp.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, August 20, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Todd Shipyards Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to plaintiff¿s claim for premises owner liability, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributed to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. As to the sophisticated user defense, assuming without deciding that some variant of the sophisticated user/intermediary defense might potentially be applicable to plaintiff¿s claim, defendant has not established either the knowledge or constructive knowledge of either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos that he was exposed to by defendant or the knowledge of defendant regarding what was known or should have been known by either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos that plaintiff was exposed to by defendant, so any such defense has not been established as a matter of law. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOE EARL DAVIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dillingham Construction'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, August 20, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Dillingham Construction¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff has presented insufficient evidence to create a triable issue regarding asbestos content of any product or material attributable to defendant to which plaintiff was exposed. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. =(220/HEK)
